channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UP MLA voluntarily asks police to fine him for riding without helmet
item.description: A video recently went viral showing Shalabh Mani Tripathi, BJP MLA from Deoria, Uttar Pradesh, riding a motorcycle without wearing a helmet. Following the incident, the MLA took the unusual step of wr...

item.title: Examining the Use of 'Fascism' in Comparing the United States, Russia, and Iran
item.description: Jack Jedwab critiques Prof. Jason Stanley's characterization of the United States as a 'fascist state' following his move from Yale University to the University of Toronto in 2025. Stanley likened his...

item.title: Shariff Aguak Mayor Seeks Swift Justice After Surviving Fifth Ambush
item.description: Shariff Aguak, Maguindanao del Sur Mayor Akmad “Mitra” Ampatuan Sr., expressed hope for a swift and favorable resolution to the complaint he filed against 15 individuals following an ambush in January...

item.title: Profile of Eli Norman, husband of journalist Janai Norman
item.description: Eli Norman is a U.S. military police veteran who served two tours in Afghanistan before moving into a civilian role within the federal government. He is married to Janai Norman, a former Good Morning ...

item.title: How the Medicare Surtax Affects Middle- and Upper-Income Households
item.description: The article discusses the 0.9% Medicare surtax, a relatively obscure tax introduced under the Affordable Care Act in 2013, which could surprise many taxpayers. This surtax applies to high earners, wit...

item.title: Boda Boda Riders Shift to Roadside Pick-Ups as Matatu Strike Hits Nairobi
item.description: On Monday, Nairobi streets saw a surge in boda boda riders abandoning ride-hailing apps and switching to traditional roadside pick-ups. This shift came as a nationwide matatu strike, called by the Tra...

item.title: Cape Town embraces curated social experiences at the Good Life Show Africa
item.description: Cape Town’s social culture is evolving from its traditional long lunches and late-night drinks towards more intentional and meaningful experiences. Residents are increasingly seeking smaller, curated ...

item.title: Western Cape Roads and Services Recover After Severe Weather
item.description: Following a period of severe weather in the Western Cape, major national routes, including the N1 and N2, have reopened and are now operating near normal capacity. Western Cape MEC for Local Governmen...

item.title: Karachi court prolongs Anmol Pinky's custody in murder investigation
item.description: In Karachi, a judicial magistrate on Monday extended the physical remand of Anmol, also known as Pinky, for another four days in connection with a murder case. Anmol had been previously arrested from ...

item.title: A Critical Reflection on Stan Grant’s Latest Book and Its Exploration of Indigenous Identity
item.description: Celeste Liddle reviews Stan Grant’s new book, reflecting on her long-term engagement with his work. Liddle notes that Grant’s career has evolved significantly; early on, he was known primarily as a jo...

item.title: Federal Shariat Court restores criminal penalty for suicide attempts, overturns 2022 law
item.description: ISLAMABAD: On Monday, the Federal Shariat Court (FSC) has declared the 2022 legislation that decriminalised suicide attempts as un-Islamic and therefore nullified it. This decision comes after hearing...

item.title: Thugs Storm APC Ondo Primaries, Delay Result Announcement
item.description: For Ondo State, drama burst for APC primary election venue on Sunday evening as some people wey dem suspect say na political thugs storm the party secretariat for Akure. Dem say the thugs fit get link...

item.title: Report Highlights Behavioral and Health Changes in President Trump as He Approaches 80
item.description: A recent report has raised concerns about President Donald Trump's fitness for office as he nears his 80th birthday, citing observable behavioral and physical changes. According to The Atlantic's Jona...

item.title: Gbenga Daniel supporters call for boycott of Ogun APC senatorial primaries over safety concerns
item.description: Supporters of former Ogun State Governor and current Ogun East Senator, Gbenga Daniel, don advise make dem no participate for the APC senatorial primaries set for Monday, May 18, 2026, for Ogun East. ...

item.title: Pamela Anderson Open to Romance Again Following Speculation Involving Tom Cruise
item.description: Pamela Anderson, the 58-year-old actress known for her iconic roles and recent career resurgence, is reportedly open to finding love once more. According to sources cited by the Daily Mail, Anderson h...

item.title: President Ruto to Convene Cabinet Meeting After Returning from Azerbaijan as Fuel Price Protests Continue
item.description: President William Ruto is currently attending the 13th World Urban Forum in Baku, Azerbaijan, where he is promoting Kenya's Affordable Housing Programme and urban economic transformation initiatives. ...

item.title: Addressing Child and Adolescent Mental Health Requires Urgent Action
item.description: South Africa, like many countries, is facing a pressing mental health challenge among children and adolescents. With over two billion young people globally, mental well-being is crucial for learning, ...

item.title: BIR Issues Guidelines on Filing Donor’s Tax for Purely Cash Donations
item.description: The Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) released Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 10-2026 to provide clear guidance on the tax treatment of purely cash donations in the Philippines. According to the...

item.title: Karachi Magistrate Delays Decision on Anmol Pinky’s Physical Remand in Murder Investigation
item.description: KARACHI: A judicial magistrate in Karachi has put on hold his verdict regarding the police request for the physical remand of Anmol, also known as Pinky, in connection with a murder case. Anmol, alleg...

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in West Africa a public health emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has officially declared the ongoing Ebola outbreak in parts of Central and East Africa a public health emergency of international concern. The outbreak, caused by t...
